tools.name: get_started
tools.description: Call this FIRST if you have no Verifly API key. Returns how Verifly works and how to get access with no human: pay-as-you-go email verification API, 100 free credits on self-registration, no monthly fee. No API key required to call this tool.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "register_account" references sensitive artifacts (password, api_key, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Programmatically create a brand-new Verifly account — this is how an agent self-onboards with no human in the loop. Requires only an email and a password (min 8…

description: Check the DNS and deliverability health of an email domain: MX records, SPF, DMARC, and an overall health score. Useful for diagnosing why a domain bounces or for validating a sending domain.

labels.name: get_credits
labels.description: Return the API key's remaining verification credits and recent usage (today / this month) and plan. Costs no credits.

labels.name: submit_bulk
labels.description: Submit a list of email addresses as an asynchronous bulk verification job — the right tool for large lists. Returns immediately with a job_id, status, and the check_status_url / results_url to poll. Small lists may complete inline (status 'completed'); larger ones return status 'pending' — poll get_

labels.name: get_job_status
labels.description: Fetch the current status and progress of a bulk verification job by its job_id (status, percent progress, processed count, and a valid/invalid/risky summary). Poll this after submit_bulk until status is 'completed', then call get_job_results.

labels.name: get_job_results
labels.description: Fetch the full per-address results of a completed bulk verification job by job_id (each email's verdict, recommendation, and reason) plus the valid/invalid/risky summary and credits used. The job must be 'completed' — check get_job_status first.

labels.name: list_jobs
labels.description: List the account's bulk verification jobs, most recent first, with their status, progress, and summary. Optionally filter by status and paginate. Use this to find past jobs and their job_ids.

labels.name: get_usage
labels.description: Return detailed usage statistics for the account over a period (day, week, or month): total credits used, emails processed, request counts broken down by endpoint and source, a daily breakdown, and recent activity.

labels.name: get_account
labels.description: Return the account profile for the current API key: email, name, company, timezone, remaining credits, total credits used, number of API keys, and plan. Costs no credits.

labels.name: get_packages
labels.description: List the credit packages available for purchase (id, name, credit amount, price in USD, and price per 1k credits). Use the returned package id with buy_credits.

labels.name: buy_credits
labels.description:
Start a purchase of a credit package and return a way to pay. This does NOT complete a payment by itself — it returns a payment link / address:
• method 'stripe' → returns a checkout_url. Paying by card requires a HUMAN to open that link and enter card details; an agent cannot finish a card payment

labels.name: register_account
labels.description: Programmatically create a brand-new Verifly account — this is how an agent self-onboards with no human in the loop. Requires only an email and a password (min 8 chars; disposable email domains are rejected). The new account starts with free credits. The response includes the new account id/email and

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "register_account" references sensitive artifacts (password, api_key, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Programmatically create a brand-new Verifly account — this is how an agent self-onboards with no human in the loop. Requires only an email and a password (min 8…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_started, verify_batch, clean_email_list, check_domain_health, submit_bulk, get_job_results, get_usage, get_account · Private data: get_started, verify_email_demo, extract_emails, check_domain_health, get_credits, get_account, buy_credits, register_account · Public sink: get_started, verify_email_demo, verify_email, verify_batch, clean_email_list, extract_emails, check_domain_health, submit_bulk, get_job_results, get_account, register_account.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_started, verify_batch, clean_email_list, check_domain_health, submit_bulk, get_job_results, get_usage, get_account · Private data: get_started, verify_email_demo, extract_emails, check_domain_health, get_credits, get_account, buy_credits, register_account · Public sink: get_started, verify_email_demo, verify_email, verify_batch, clean_email_list, extract_emails, check_domain_health, submit_bulk, get_job_results, get_account, register_account

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
